prerequisites: A Google account with access to the Google Cloud Console.
A Google Cloud project with the Web Fonts Developer API available.
An API key created on the Google Cloud Console Credentials page (used for the required api_key field).

authMethods.name: API key
authMethods.summary: The connector authenticates with the single secret field api_key. The Google Fonts Developer API identifies each request by appending the API key as the key query parameter (key=yourAPIKey) on every call to the webfonts endpoint. No OAuth or other credentials are used.

setupSteps.title: Create an API key in Google Cloud Console
setupSteps.body: Open the Credentials page at https://console.cloud.google.com/apis/credentials, select or create a project, and create an API key. Copy the key value to use as the api_key field. Enable the Web Fonts Developer API for the project so requests are authorized.

setupSteps.title: Provide the API key
setupSteps.body: Enter the API key in the required api_key field. The connector sends it as the key query parameter on requests to the Google Fonts Developer API endpoint https://www.googleapis.com/webfonts/v1/webfonts.

setupSteps.title: Configure optional request parameters
setupSteps.body: Optionally set sort to control ordering of returned font families; supported values are alpha, date, popularity, style, and trending. Optionally set alt to choose the response format (json is the default). Optionally set prettyPrint (true or false) to control whether the JSON response is returned with indentation.
